vulnerability.reference: Over-reliance on generative outputs without orthogonal experimental validation due to time and cost pressures in drug development pipelines.
vulnerability.generated: Biomedical researchers lack means to detect subtle distributional biases in flow-model outputs, relying on generated sequences without exact distribution guarantees.

impact.reference: Advancement of non-viable therapeutic candidates into costly preclinical testing, wasting resources and delaying effective treatments.
impact.generated: Non-viable or hazardous protein therapeutics advance through development, wasting resources and endangering patient safety.

dose_response.reference: Undetectable distributional biases in flow-generated sequences enable subtly flawed therapeutic candidates, which in protein design scenarios bypass expert scrutiny, leading to advancement of non-viable candidates into costly preclinical testing.
dose_response.generated: Discrete flow models’ hard-to-detect distributional biases, through biomedical researchers designing protein sequences with them, trigger reliance on outputs without exact distribution guarantees, leading to non-viable or hazardous therapeutics advancing, wasting resources and endangering patient safety.
